entities.name: alpha

evidence.quote: In the financial world, there's this concept of alpha.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Alpha is outperformance relative to the index.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So alpha is the outperformance relative to the index.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So your ideal stock is a very high alpha, very low beta stock.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: They don't really come in that shape because alpha and beta tend to be correlated, but that's what you want when you buy a financial asset.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I think you have high alpha people who are very valuable.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So this alpha beta thing, I use it pretty often when contemplating what kind of person I want and also what kind of process I want.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So when you're building a product from zero to one, you're probably pursuing alpha.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: The downside of a process is that it suppresses alpha.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And you have to be super, super careful and judicious in the application of process and the product team to know that you're lowering beta in the places where you want to do that without suppressing alpha in the places where you need it.

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 幂律 (power law)
entities.how_described: 商业结果遵循的分布规律，即少数顶尖的事物占据绝大部分回报，嘉宾借此强调必须保持极致的努力才能触及高回报区。

evidence.quote: There's a topic around this area that I wanted to spend time on, and I haven't heard anyone think of things this way, which is this idea you talk about of compounding plus power law plus entropy and how that's a really useful frame to think about business.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Man, understanding the nature of the universe is a pretty good way to work within it, and so power law distributions happen everywhere.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: It's like, power law distribution just plays out everywhere, and once you see it, you can't unsee it.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: You haven't hit the inflection point in terms of reward, and so the implication of the power law more broadly is that people who are in the top 10%, the top 5%, don't just get 10 or 20% more reward.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Your job is to preserve that intensity at its highest possible level and let the buffering happen somewhere else, and that's the point is that entropy creeps into the system insidiously and slowly over time off your radar and you have to maintain that intensity every minute of every day to try and fight it if you want to stay at the extreme right end of the power law that obviously governs the outcome of everything that we build.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 熵 (entropy)
entities.how_described: 系统中自然走向混乱的趋势，嘉宾强调高管和领导者的重要职责就是持续注入能量来对抗熵增，防止组织衰退。

evidence.quote: There's a topic around this area that I wanted to spend time on, and I haven't heard anyone think of things this way, which is this idea you talk about of compounding plus power law plus entropy and how that's a really useful frame to think about business.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Entropy, the second law of thermodynamics is a very simple concept.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: If you abandon a city for a few months, it starts to go fallow, and so entropy is just this concept that shit tends toward disorder.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: The universe, I mean, life itself is a temporary victory against entropy.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: It organizes stuff that we can eat and we fight entropy until we lose the battle somewhere, as I said earlier at the age of 70 or 80, if we're lucky.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: The only antidote to entropy, the only antidote to decay in a system is energy.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So if you have a code base, every line of code that you add to that code base increases the entropy of that system and demands ever more energy from human beings to go and intend to it to make sure it doesn't break, and if you want to achieve greatness, if you want an extraordinary outcome, and in particular, if you want to be in the top 10%, top 5% on the X axis so that the Y axis is through the roof, then you have to relentlessly inject energy at every single step of the game.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Not because they get together and think, "We should do that," although unions do do that unequivocally, deliberately, but even in a collection of product managers or engineers, what's going to happen over time is entropy is going to creep in and people are going to optimize for local comfort.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Your job as an executive, as a leader, is to fight that entropy tooth and nail every single day.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: It means that every time you see a bug, every time you see a bug, not most of the time, every time you go and you drop it at the feet of the product manager or the engineering manager, every time, in public, preferably, it means that every time someone comes to you to hire someone and says that they have skipped the back channel, every time you're like, "You can't hire this person unless you do the back channel," it means that when you get into the board and tired zone on any process, that you as the executive have got to demand the 99th percentile of energy, because otherwise entropy creeps in and the system decays.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Your job is to preserve that intensity at its highest possible level and let the buffering happen somewhere else, and that's the point is that entropy creeps into the system insidiously and slowly over time off your radar and you have to maintain that intensity every minute of every day to try and fight it if you want to stay at the extreme right end of the power law that obviously governs the outcome of everything that we build.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 产品市场契合度 (product market fit)
entities.how_described: 嘉宾用「药物发现与受体结合」的绝妙比喻来形容它，强调市场需求是预先存在的，如果四五年内仍未迎来爆发式增长，就应该果断重置。

evidence.quote: You touched on this product market fit point, and I want to double down on this thread.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: You've had a couple really interesting experiences of struggling to find product market fit with your own startup.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And then with Rippling, complete opposite, extreme product market fit, up and to the right.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: What's something you've learned about just that that you think people maybe don't understand about what it feels like, what it takes to get to product market fit, how things change?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: There's a line that this venture capitalist, whose name I will not mention, said, which was that product market fit is a sort of thing where you absolutely know it when you see it, and therefore if you don't absolutely know it, you don't have it.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: We thought we had product market fit, maybe, maybe.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And in hindsight, with the benefit of now having experienced solid product market fit, it was so, so obvious that we didn't.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: You should reset the clock, you should reset the cap table because trust me, product market fit when it arrives is insane and it's exciting and you should pursue it.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I think Rippling, had it been started today, would not be what it is five years from now today, and so I think timing is a lot and it's very hard to control for, but when you get the timing right and the market is real and the product works, product market fit, like I said earlier, it's super clear, and so if I were to pick a number out of a hat just from my lived experience, I think it's very important, one aside, don't ask people for advice, ask people for relevant experience.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: You have this really fun way of describing product market fit around receptors and drugs.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: How do you think about the insignificance of your launch or you think about all the effort you're putting into building a product that you believe is going to have product market fit?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 单点解决方案 (point solutions)
entities.how_described: 只解决某一个特定问题的 SaaS 软件。嘉宾指出在 AI 时代它们因缺乏全局数据而陷入绝境，只能「用吸管喝数据」。

evidence.quote: And SaaS might be dead-ish in terms of point solutions, but long live SaaS in terms of what we're building.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So here's the problem; point solutions don't have enough data in the age of AI to be useful.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: The other thing that I would say about the world of, even just like not SaaS, but AI software is that point solutions in the AI world are also in a rough spot for the same reason.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0
